evidence: Java has no module-level import-time side effects in the Python sense. No static initializers with I/O, DB connections, or network calls found in any class

BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor performs file I/O (opens BlackLab index). Javadoc explicitly notes: 'Constructor I/O note: opening the index is inherent to BlackLab's lifecycle model — the index must be open before any query can execute' — documented as intentional by design, not accidental coupling
BlackLabQueryExecutor declares throws IOException on its constructor so callers receive a checked exception if the index is missing or corrupt — the I/O coupling is surfaced, not hidden

CollocateQueryHelper accepts a @Nullable BlackLabIndex in its constructor specifically to enable test subclasses — this is a testability seam that avoids forcing tests to open a real index. The null is documented and guarded with Objects.requireNonNull at method level

GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish() reads System.getProperty('grammar.config', 'grammars/relations.json') at call time — not at class load time. This allows per-test overrides via System.setProperty and is consistent with how CORS origin is also read at call time in HttpApiUtils

WordSketchApiServer constructor builds handler objects but defers port binding to start() — this separation means construction does not open sockets. Comment on line 69 documents this: 'Port binding is deferred to start() so that construction and network binding are separated'

SemanticFieldExplorer constructor derives nounCqlConstraint from GrammarConfig at construction time — this is a pure computation over an already-loaded config object, not I/O or network. The derived value is stable and correct as a constructor-time computation
CollocateProfileComparator constructor similarly derives adjectivePattern from GrammarConfig at construction — same pattern, same rationale

Main.handleBlackLabServerCommand registers a shutdown hook before constructing the server: 'Register the shutdown hook before constructing the server so the executor is always closed even if server construction throws' — comment shows awareness of initialization ordering
No global singletons with cross-class initialization order dependencies detected. Each class receives its dependencies via constructor injection

strengths: No static initializers with side effects anywhere in the codebase
System property reads deferred to call time (not class load time) — CORS origin, grammar config path both read at call time, enabling test overrides
Port binding separated from server construction in WordSketchApiServer
Shutdown hook registration ordered before server construction in Main to ensure cleanup even on construction failure
Constructor injection throughout — no hidden service locator or global mutable state
initialization_coupling.issue_character: The only initialization concern is BlackLabQueryExecutor performing file I/O in the constructor, which is documented as intentional. There are no circular init dependencies, import-time side effects, or boot-order hazards.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: Java's compilation model eliminates most import-time side effect risks present in Python/Node codebases. This codebase is further disciplined: system property reads are deferred to call time, port binding is separated from construction, and constructor injection prevents hidden initialization ordering. The BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor I/O is the only pattern that could complicate testing, and it is explicitly handled via the null-accepting CollocateQueryHelper constructor. Scoring at 84 reflects strong initialization discipline with one intentional, documented constructor I/O coupling.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor opens the BlackLab index file in its constructor, making it impossible to construct without a valid index path
evidence: Line 58: public BlackLabQueryExecutor(String indexPath) throws IOException — constructor opens file
Line 61: this.blackLabIndex = BlackLab.open(new File(indexPath)) — file I/O in constructor

Tests requiring BlackLabQueryExecutor behavior must either have a real index available or create a subclass/mock — BlackLabQueryExecutorTest uses Assumptions.assumeTrue to skip live-index tests when no index is provided
issues.suggestion: The current design is documented as intentional and appropriate for BlackLab's lifecycle model. If future requirements call for deferred opening (e.g., lazy initialization or health-check mode), consider a static factory: BlackLabQueryExecutor.open(String indexPath) that performs the I/O, keeping the constructor private and side-effect-free. For now, the existing design with the documented rationale is acceptable.

root_causes: Constructor I/O in BlackLabQueryExecutor is a deliberate design decision driven by BlackLab's resource lifecycle, not accidental coupling
